title: Modifying Glyphs
text: Modifying Glyphs You cannot modify a glyph directly in Uniface. You must first export the image to a file and modify it using your preferred image editor, before replacing the glyph in Uniface. For example, you can create different language variations of the same glyph, or create both a color and monochrome version. In the Glyph editor, select the glyph and choose Edit > Unload . The glyph is copied to a bitmap file in the IDE's project directory (unless otherwise specified). If the glyph contains a secondary image, the image is unloaded as two files—the primary image file with the .bmp file extension, and the secondary image file with the .amp file extension. The original glyph remains unchanged in the Repository. Modify the image file using your preferred image editor. To replace a glyph, delete the original glyph object and create a new glyph object with the same name. To create a new variation, select the original glyph object and create a new glyph item.
